title: VimFu — Ctrl-G U Don't Break Undo
description: Ctrl-G U followed by a motion prevents the next cursor movement from breaking the undo sequence.

setup.comment: Set TERM for proper color support in Neovim

steps.say: Control G capital U tells Vim that the next cursor motion should not break the undo sequence. Normally, arrow keys in insert mode create undo breaks. This prevents that.
This is an advanced technique. Some people map it so that arrow keys in insert mode don't break undo. The idea is: keep your entire insert session as one undoable action, even if you move around.
Without this, pressing left or right arrow in insert mode starts a new undo block. Control G capital U before the motion keeps it all together.
Control G capital U. Prevent cursor motions from breaking undo. An advanced tweak for custom insert mode mappings.
